blockingIssues.description: Duplicate-named sandbox agents are keyed only by `(agent.name, sandbox_agent_signature)`, so structurally identical agents with the same name collapse onto one sandbox identity/runtime and can leak workspace state across resume and composition flows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `rust-sandbox-worker` guidance or required regression coverage to include an adversarial case for duplicate-named sandbox agents that are structurally identical, not just duplicate names with differing manifests, models, or instructions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The scrutiny review for `sandbox-handoffs-tools-and-identity-parity` found that the landed regression only exercises duplicate `sandbox` agents with different instructions/manifests/models in `crates/openai-agents/tests/sandbox_semantics.rs`, which allowed an identity-collision bug for structurally identical duplicate-named agents to pass unnoticed.
